=== templates/active_orders.html ===
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Active Orders</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="active-orders-page">
        <h1>Active Orders</h1>
        <label for="status-filter">Filter by status:</label>
        <select id="status-filter" onchange="filterOrders()">
            <option value="All" {% if status_filter == 'All' %}selected{% endif %}>All</option>
            <option value="Preparing" {% if status_filter == 'Preparing' %}selected{% endif %}>Preparing</option>
            <option value="On the Way" {% if status_filter == 'On the Way' %}selected{% endif %}>On the Way</option>
            <option value="Delivered" {% if status_filter == 'Delivered' %}selected{% endif %}>Delivered</option>
        </select>
        <div id="orders-list">
            {% for o in orders %}
            <div class="order-entry" data-status="{{ o.status }}">
                <p>Order ID: {{ o.order_id }}</p>
                <p>Restaurant: {{ o.restaurant_name }}</p>
                <p>Status: {{ o.status }}</p>
                <p>ETA: {{ o.delivery_time if o.delivery_time else 'Unknown' }}</p>
                <button id="track-order-button-{{ o.order_id }}" onclick="location.href='/tracking/' + {{ o.order_id }}">Track Order</button>
            </div>
            {% endfor %}
        </div>
        <button id="back-to-dashboard" onclick="location.href='/'">Back to Dashboard</button>
    </div>
    <script>
    function filterOrders() {
        var filter = document.getElementById('status-filter').value;
        var orders = document.getElementsByClassName('order-entry');
        for (var i = 0; i < orders.length; i++) {
            var status = orders[i].getAttribute('data-status');
            if (filter == 'All' || status == filter) {
                orders[i].style.display = '';
            } else {
                orders[i].style.display = 'none';
            }
        }
    }
    </script>
</body>
</html>

=== templates/restaurants.html ===
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Browse Restaurants</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="restaurants-page">
        <h1>Browse Restaurants</h1>
        <input id="search-input" type="text" placeholder="Search by name or cuisine" value="{{ search_query }}" oninput="filterRestaurants()" />
        <select id="cuisine-filter" onchange="filterRestaurants()">
            <option value="all" {% if cuisine_filter == 'all' %}selected{% endif %}>All</option>
            <option value="Chinese" {% if cuisine_filter == 'Chinese' %}selected{% endif %}>Chinese</option>
            <option value="Italian" {% if cuisine_filter == 'Italian' %}selected{% endif %}>Italian</option>
            <option value="Indian" {% if cuisine_filter == 'Indian' %}selected{% endif %}>Indian</option>
            <option value="American" {% if cuisine_filter == 'American' %}selected{% endif %}>American</option>
        </select>
        <div id="restaurants-grid">
            {% for r in restaurants %}
                <div class="restaurant-card" data-name="{{ r.name }}" data-cuisine="{{ r.cuisine }}">
                    <h2>{{ r.name }}</h2>
                    <p>Cuisine: {{ r.cuisine }}</p>
                    <p>Address: {{ r.address }}</p>
                    <p>Phone: {{ r.phone }}</p>
                    <p>Rating: {{ r.rating }}</p>
                    <p>Delivery Time: {{ r.delivery_time }} mins</p>
                    <p>Minimum Order: ${{ '%.2f'|format(r.min_order) }}</p>
                    <button id="view-restaurant-button-{{ r.restaurant_id }}" onclick="location.href='/restaurant/{{ r.restaurant_id }}'">View Menu</button>
                </div>
            {% endfor %}
        </div>
    </div>
    <script>
        function filterRestaurants() {
            var filter = document.getElementById('cuisine-filter').value;
            var search = document.getElementById('search-input').value.toLowerCase();
            var cards = document.getElementsByClassName('restaurant-card');
            for (var i = 0; i < cards.length; i++) {
                var name = cards[i].getAttribute('data-name').toLowerCase();
                var cuisine = cards[i].getAttribute('data-cuisine');
                var matchesSearch = name.includes(search) || cuisine.toLowerCase().includes(search);
                if ((filter === 'all' || cuisine === filter) && matchesSearch) {
                    cards[i].style.display = '';
                } else {
                    cards[i].style.display = 'none';
                }
            }
        }
    </script>
</body>
</html>
=== templates/reviews.html ===
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <title>Order Reviews</title>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="reviews-page">
        <h1>Order Reviews</h1>
        <label for="filter-by-rating">Filter by rating:</label>
        <select id="filter-by-rating" onchange="filterReviews()">
            <option value="All" {% if rating_filter == 'All' %}selected{% endif %}>All</option>
            <option value="5" {% if rating_filter == '5' %}selected{% endif %}>5 Stars</option>
            <option value="4" {% if rating_filter == '4' %}selected{% endif %}>4 Stars</option>
            <option value="3" {% if rating_filter == '3' %}selected{% endif %}>3 Stars</option>
            <option value="2" {% if rating_filter == '2' %}selected{% endif %}>2 Stars</option>
            <option value="1" {% if rating_filter == '1' %}selected{% endif %}>1 Star</option>
        </select>
        <div id="reviews-list">
            {% for rev in reviews %}
            <div class="review-entry" data-rating="{{ rev.rating }}">
                <p><strong>Restaurant:</strong> {{ rev.restaurant_name }}</p>
                <p><strong>Customer:</strong> {{ rev.customer_name }}</p>
                <p><strong>Rating:</strong> {{ rev.rating }}</p>
                <p><strong>Review:</strong> {{ rev.review_text }}</p>
                <p><em>Date: {{ rev.review_date }}</em></p>
            </div>
            {% endfor %}
        </div>
        <button id="write-review-button" onclick="alert('Write review feature not implemented yet')">Write Review</button>
        <button id="back-to-dashboard" onclick="location.href='/'">Back to Dashboard</button>
    </div>
    <script>
        function filterReviews() {
            var filter = document.getElementById('filter-by-rating').value;
            var reviews = document.getElementsByClassName('review-entry');
            for (var i = 0; i < reviews.length; i++) {
                var rating = parseInt(reviews[i].getAttribute('data-rating'));
                if (filter === 'All' || rating == parseInt(filter)) {
                    reviews[i].style.display = '';
                } else {
                    reviews[i].style.display = 'none';
                }
            }
        }
    </script>
</body>
</html>

=== Agent Output ===
=== code_feedback.md ===
NEED_MODIFY

1. Template 'orders.html' is found corrupted and contains invalid/unreadable content, potentially a leftover or unrelated file: it should be removed or fixed if intended to be a functional page. According to design_spec.md, there is no separate Orders page - only Active Orders and Tracking pages, so it appears unnecessary.

2. In 'item_details' route (app.py), POST method reads quantity from form field 'quantity-input'; however, the 'item_details.html' template name attribute for the quantity input is 'quantity' without the '-input' suffix. This leads to the quantity field always defaulting to 1 on add to cart from item details page. This mismatch should be corrected by either:
- Changing name attribute in item_details.html input to 'quantity-input' (recommended for consistency with app.py).
- Or changing app.py to read 'quantity' instead of 'quantity-input'.

3. In the shopping cart page template (cart.html), the button with id 'proceed-checkout-button' is a button with an onclick JavaScript redirect to '/checkout'. However, in app.py POST handler for '/cart', POST request checks for 'proceed-checkout-button' in form data to detect proceed checkout action, which will never happen since this button does not submit the form but redirects via onclick.
  This inconsistency means the server never processes the proceed checkout action through POST form submission.
  Possible fixes:
  - Change the button to a submit button within the form with that ID and name, so POST sends proper form data.
  - Or handle proceed checkout only with client-side redirect as is, removing server-side POST check for proceed.

4. Minor: In 'active_orders.html' template, the order entry shows "ETA: {{ o.delivery_time if o.delivery_time else 'Unknown' }}". The delivery_time attribute does not exist in order objects loaded in app.py filtered list or added. This will cause the ETA always to show 'Unknown'.
  If ETA should be shown, the system must fetch delivery estimated time from deliveries data and join it to orders, or omit ETA field.

Summary:
- Remove or fix corrupted 'orders.html'.
- Fix quantity input name mismatch in item_details post processing.
- Align proceed-checkout-button action in shopping cart form between frontend and backend.
- Fix or remove ETA display in active orders page.

No syntax/runtime errors detected otherwise. All required pages and element IDs are implemented correctly.

Evaluate the above output based on the agent's role and task requirements.


OUTPUT: {"result": "CONTINUE", "rationale": "The feedback correctly identifies critical issues and inconsistencies between the app.py and templates, including a corrupted template file, form input name mismatches, inconsistent button behavior in the cart page, and missing data for ETA display in active orders page. It also confirms that there are no syntax or runtime errors and that all other pages and element IDs comply with design_spec.md. The feedback is detailed, actionable, and aligned with the agent role requirements.", "feedback": "The feedback provides the necessary corrections for quantity input name mismatch, cart proceed checkout button behavior, corrupted orders.html removal, and ETA display fix. These adjustments will ensure functional user interactions and consistency across the app."}
